name: French Toast Stuffed with Bananas and Walnuts
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large, shallow bowl, beat the eggs with a fork until lightly frothy.
2. Stir in the milk and set aside.
3. Peel the bananas and mash them with a fork. Stir in the walnuts and nutmeg.
4. Spread the banana-walnut mixture evenly over half of each bread slice, leaving a 1/4 inch border on all edges.
5. Top with remaining bread slices and gently press to seal.
6. Place two sandwiches in the egg mixture. Turn gently until both sides are evenly saturated.
7. Remove from the bowl and repeat with the remaining sandwiches.
8. Melt 2 tablespoons butter in a frying pan or griddle over medium heat. Add the sandwiches, basting them occasionally with additional butter, until golden brown on both sides, about 2 minutes per side.
9. Place on warmed plates. Dust lightly with confectioners' sugar using a fine mesh sieve.
10. Serve hot with jam or maple syrup alongside.
Notes:
Layering two slices of bread, sandwich style, with a mixture of bananas and walnuts creates French toast with a surprise filling. Try other favorite nuts instead of walnuts if desired. Accompany with bacon, ham, or sausage.
